title: Grief and acute-stress support boundary
summary: Offer safety, calm presence, practical help, water, privacy, contact with trusted people, and clear information without forcing discussion.
content: Offer safety, calm presence, practical help, water, privacy, contact with trusted people, and clear information without forcing discussion. Seek urgent professional help when there is immediate danger, inability to care for basic needs, severe confusion, violence, or risk of self-harm. Do not promise outcomes, demand a detailed retelling, or use alcohol or unprescribed medicine as emotional first aid.
materials: Notebook or printed card. Context: “Grief and acute-stress support boundary”.
Pencil. Context: “Grief and acute-stress support boundary”.

when_not_to_use: Do not use this card to override an evacuation order, emergency responder, clinician, product label, or local law for Grief and acute-stress support boundary.
short_term: For Grief and acute-stress support boundary, stabilize the immediate risk, communicate the decision, and schedule the next check before attention moves elsewhere.
long_term: After Grief and acute-stress support boundary, update supplies, contact lists, maps, training, and the written plan from what actually happened.
if_method_fails: Stop Grief and acute-stress support boundary, isolate the unsafe result, communicate the failure, and use the safest official or professionally supported fallback available.
environment_notes: Adapt Grief and acute-stress support boundary to Greek heat, wildfire smoke, earthquakes, islands, mountain roads, apartment stairs, power cuts, water interruptions, mobility limits, and official alerts.
